title: Advanced Stochastic Processes & Distribution Theory

prerequisites.text: STA2004F, STA2005S and MAM2010F, MAM2011F, MAM2012S and MAM2014S. Concurrent registration for STA3041F required. Note: A student may not register concurrently for STA3045F and CSC2001F. This course is for students studying Actuarial Science or Mathematical Statistics, though not a requirement for a major in Mathematical Statistics.

outline: This course begins with a brief introduction to copulas and extreme value theory, together with applications to risk management. The rest of the course gives a theoretical overview of stochastic processes, with models spanning both discrete and continuous time as well as discrete and continuous state-space. Though the emphasis is on theoretical properties of the models, real-world applications are also explored. Topics covered: copulas, extreme value theory, homogenous and non-homogeneous continuous-time Markov chains, random walks, probability theory, martingales, Brownian motion, and diffusion processes.
lecture_times: Five lectures per week, Monday - Friday, 2nd period.
dp_requirements: Satisfactory attendance of lectures, tutorials, practicals and tests and completion of assignments and/or class exercises as set out in course outline. Class record of at least 35%.
assessment: Class record 30% and a 3-hour exam counting 70%. Weights will be adjusted in the case of missed assessments, as detailed in the course outline.
